paper_title: Lipid analysis of T. weissflogii and C. cryptica under nutrient limitation

methods.description: Determination of total lipid content in lipid extracts by weighing.

steps.description: Lipid content (mg L −1 ) was determined gravimetrically by weighting lipid extracts.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate lipid content by weighing lipid extracts.

methods.method_name: Lipid Class Composition Estimation by Silica Gel Chromatography
methods.description: Estimation of the percentage of different lipid classes (free fatty acids, triglycerides, glycolipids, and phospholipids) after purification on a silica gel column.

steps.description: Lipid composition (percentage of free fatty acids, triglycerides, glycolipids, and phospholipids) was estimated after purification on silica gel column of each class of molecules.
steps.searchable_summary: Purify lipid extracts using silica gel column chromatography to separate lipid classes.
outputs: Separated lipid classes (free fatty acids, triglycerides, glycolipids, phospholipids)

steps.description: (Implicit step based on the description) Estimate the percentage of each purified lipid class.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate the percentage of each separated lipid class.

methods.method_name: Total Fatty Acid Composition Analysis by GC-MS
methods.description: Determination of the total fatty acid composition of lipid extracts by analyzing fatty acid methyl esters (FAME) using Gas Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry (GC-MS) after saponification.

steps.description: Total fatty acid composition was determined by GC-MS on the corresponding methyl esters (fatty acid methyl ester (FAME)) obtained after saponification of lipid extracts with Na 2 CO 3 in MeOH (42°C) for 4 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Saponify lipid extracts with Na2CO3 in MeOH at 42°C for 4 hours to produce FAME.

steps.description: Pentadecanoic acid was used as internal standard.
steps.searchable_summary: Add pentadecanoic acid as an internal standard to the FAME sample.

steps.description: FAME mixtures were analyzed with a gas chromatograph equipped with a mass spectrometer (Focus GC-PolarisQ, Thermo Fisher Scientific, Waltham, MA, USA).
(injector 260°C; detector 260°C; temperature gradient 160°C up to 260°C, 5°C/min). Set GC injector temperature to 260°C.
(injector 260°C; detector 260°C; temperature gradient 160°C up to 260°C, 5°C/min). Set GC detector temperature to 260°C.
(injector 260°C; detector 260°C; temperature gradient 160°C up to 260°C, 5°C/min). Apply GC temperature gradient 160°C up to 260°C at 5°C/min.

methods.method_name: Protein Content Determination by Lowry Method
methods.description: Determination of protein content in culture samples using the Lowry method with a commercial assay kit.

steps.description: Protein content was determined by Lowry method (RC DC protein assay; Bio-Rad) on 20 mL of culture.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect 20 mL culture sample for protein analysis.

steps.description: Protein content was determined by Lowry method (RC DC protein assay; Bio-Rad) on 20 mL of culture.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Lowry protein assay using Bio-Rad RC DC kit on culture sample.

methods.method_name: Carbohydrate Content Determination by Phenol-Sulfuric Acid Method
methods.description: Determination of carbohydrate content in culture samples using the phenol-sulfuric acid method.

steps.description: Carbohydrate content was determined using the phenol-sulfuric acid method [48] on 100 mL of culture.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect 100 mL culture sample for carbohydrate analysis.

steps.description: Carbohydrate content was determined using the phenol-sulfuric acid method [48] on 100 mL of culture.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform phenol-sulfuric acid assay on culture sample to determine carbohydrate content.
